tool.name: jdeskew

configfiles.configfile:
import cv2 import numpy as np from jdeskew.estimator import get_angle from jdeskew.utility import rotate
with open('${input_image}', 'rb') as fh: content = fh.read()
nparr = np.frombuffer(content, np.uint8) image = cv2.imdecode(nparr, cv2.IMREAD_COLOR) angle = get_angle(image, angle_max=${angle_max}) output_image = rotate(image, angle, resize=${resize})
cv2.imwrite('./out.${output_image.ext}', output_image)
with open('${output_angle}', 'w') as fh: fh.write(f'{angle}\n')

param.label: Maximum detectable skew angle (degrees)
param.help: Skew angles beyond this value will not be corrected.

param.label: Resize output to fit rotated content
param.help: When enabled, the output canvas expands to avoid cropping corners. When disabled, the original dimensions are kept and corners are filled with black.

tool.help:
**jdeskew** estimates and corrects the skew angle of a document image.
Skew is introduced when a document is placed at a slight angle on a scanner or photographed off-axis. Correcting it improves the accuracy of downstream OCR and layout analysis.
-----
**Inputs**
- *Input image* — A scanned or photographed document in PNG, JPEG, or TIFF format.
**Parameters**
- *Maximum detectable skew angle* — Skew angles larger than this threshold are ignored. Lower values speed up processing and avoid misidentifying non-document rotations as skew. Default: 45°. - *Resize output* — When enabled, the output canvas grows to contain the full rotated image. When disabled, the image is cropped to the original dimensions and unfilled corners are black.
**Outputs**
- *Deskewed image* — The corrected image in the same format as the input. - *Detected angle* — A plain-text file containing the estimated skew angle in degrees (negative = counter-clockwise tilt).
-----
**Method**
jdeskew uses Adaptive Radial Projection on the Fourier Magnitude Spectrum (ARPFMS) to estimate the dominant text-line angle without requiring binarisation or line detection.
